Risk Factors
  • jump to section"There is no guarantee that we will have sufficient cash flow from our business to pay our indebtedness or that we will not incur additional indebtedness."
  • jump to section"If we are unable to generate such cash flow, we may be required to adopt one or more alternatives, such as reducing or delaying investments or capital expenditures, selling assets, refinancing or obtaining additional equity capital on terms that may be onerous or highly dilutive."
  • jump to section"Our business may not continue to generate cash flow from operations in the future sufficient to satisfy our obligations under our existing indebtedness and any future indebtedness we may incur, and to make necessary capital expenditures."
Q2 2021
View TranscriptView 10-K/10-Q

What management said

  • "With strong operating cash flows and cash balance, we are putting that cash to use."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"Net cash provided by operating activities $ 3,765 $ 524"

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A
  • jump to section"Our operating cash inflows include cash from vehicle sales, customer lease payments, customer deposits, cash from sales of regulatory credits and energy generation and storage products."
  • jump to section"Summary of Cash Flows Six Months Ended June 30, (Dollars in millions) 2021 2020 Net cash provided by operating activities $ 3,765 $ 524 Net cash used in investing activities $ (4,097 ) $ (1,046 ) Net cash (used in) provided by financing activities $ (2,565 ) $ 2,831 Cash Flows from Operating Activities Our cash flows from operating activities are significantly affected by our cash investments to support the growth of our business in areas such as research and development and selling, general and administrative and working capital, especially inventory, which includes vehicles in transit."
  • jump to section"Cash Flows from Financing Activities Net cash used in financing activities during the six months ended June 30, 2021 was $2.57 billion, which consisted primarily of $1.95 billion of cash repayments upon conversions of our convertible senior notes, $614 million of repayments under our Fixed Asset Facility, $294 million of repayments under our 2016 Warehouse Agreement, $151 million repayment of Solar Term Loan upon maturity and $196 million principal repayments of our finance leases."
  • jump to section"Our business has recently been consistently generating cash flow from operations in excess of our level of capital spend, and with better working capital management resulting in shorter days sales outstanding than days payable outstanding, our sales growth is also facilitating positive cash generation."
  • jump to section"40 Liquidity and Capital Resources We expect to continue to generate net positive operating cash flow as we have done in the last three fiscal years."
  • jump to section"Cash Flow and Capital Expenditure Trends Our capital expenditures are typically difficult to project beyond the short term given the number and breadth of our core projects at any given time, and may further be impacted by uncertainties in future global market conditions."

Risk Factors
  • jump to section"52 There is no guarantee that we will have sufficient cash flow from our business to pay our indebtedness or that we will not incur additional indebtedness."
  • jump to section"If we are unable to generate such cash flow, we may be required to adopt one or more alternatives, such as reducing or delaying investments or capital expenditures, selling assets, refinancing or obtaining additional equity capital on terms that may be onerous or highly dilutive."
  • jump to section"Our business may not continue to generate cash flow from operations in the future sufficient to satisfy our obligations under our existing indebtedness and any future indebtedness we may incur, and to make necessary capital expenditures."
Q3 2021
View TranscriptView 10-K/10-Q

What management said

No high-confidence mentions this period - showing weaker, lower-confidence matches instead:

  • "On cash, we generated record operating cash flows of $3.1 billion and continued to invest heavily in the build-out of manufacturing, supercharging, and service capacity."
  • "Please keep in mind that given backlog, it will take time for the impact of recent changes to flow through our financials."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"Net cash provided by operating activities $ 6,912 $ 2,924"

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A
  • jump to section"Our operating cash inflows include cash from vehicle sales, customer lease payments, customer deposits, cash from sales of regulatory credits and energy generation and storage products."
  • jump to section"40 Summary of Cash Flows Nine Months Ended September 30, (Dollars in millions) 2021 2020 Net cash provided by operating activities $ 6,912 $ 2,924 Net cash used in investing activities $ (5,952 ) $ (2,085 ) Net cash (used in) provided by financing activities $ (3,946 ) $ 7,281 Cash Flows from Operating Activities Our cash flows from operating activities are significantly affected by our cash investments to support the growth of our business in areas such as research and development and selling, general and administrative and working capital, especially inventory, which includes vehicles in transit."
  • jump to section"Cash Flows from Financing Activities Net cash used in financing activities during the nine months ended September 30, 2021 was $3.95 billion, which consisted primarily of $5.14 billion of net repayments under our various debt facilities and $311 million principal repayments of our finance leases ."
  • jump to section"Our business has recently been consistently generating cash flow from operations in excess of our level of capital spend, and with better working capital management resulting in shorter days sales outstanding than days payable outstanding, our sales growth is also facilitating positive cash generation."
  • jump to section"Liquidity and Capital Resources We expect to continue to generate net positive operating cash flow as we have done in the last three fiscal years."
  • jump to section"Cash Flow and Capital Expenditure Trends Our capital expenditures are typically difficult to project beyond the short term given the number and breadth of our core projects at any given time, and may further be impacted by uncertainties in future global market conditions."

Risk Factors
  • jump to section"There is no guarantee that we will have sufficient cash flow from our business to pay our indebtedness or that we will not incur additional indebtedness."
  • jump to section"If we are unable to generate such cash flow, we may be required to adopt one or more alternatives, such as reducing or delaying investments or capital expenditures, selling assets, refinancing or obtaining additional equity capital on terms that may be onerous or highly dilutive."
  • jump to section"Our business may not continue to generate cash flow from operations in the future sufficient to satisfy our obligations under our existing indebtedness and any future indebtedness we may incur, and to make necessary capital expenditures."
Q4 2021
View TranscriptView 10-K/10-Q

What management said

  • "Our free cash flows have remained strong, reaching record levels in Q4 of $2.8 billion, despite increased CapEx."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"Net cash provided by operating activities $ 11,497 $ 5,943 $ 2,405"
  • "Our free cash flows have remained strong, reaching record levels in Q4 of $2.8 billion, despite increased CapEx."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A
  • jump to section"Our operating cash inflows include cash from vehicle sales and related servicing, customer lease payments, customer deposits, cash from sales of regulatory credits and energy generation and storage products."
  • jump to section"Cash Flows from Financing Activities Cash outflows from financing activities were $5.20 billion during the year ended December 31, 2021 compared to $9.97 billion net cash provided by financing activities during the year ended December 31, 2020."
  • jump to section"Our business has recently been consistently generating cash flow from operations in excess of our level of capital spend, and with better working capital management resulting in shorter days sales outstanding than days payable outstanding, our sales growth is also facilitating positive cash generation."
  • jump to section"Liquidity and Capital Resources We expect to continue to generate net positive operating cash flow as we have done in the last four fiscal years."
  • jump to section"Cash Flow and Capital Expenditure Trends Our capital expenditures are typically difficult to project beyond the short term given the number and breadth of our core projects at any given time, and may further be impacted by uncertainties in future global market conditions."

Risk Factors
  • jump to section"There is no guarantee that we will have sufficient cash flow from our business to pay our indebtedness or that we will not incur additional indebtedness."
  • jump to section"If we are unable to generate such cash flow, we may be required to adopt one or more alternatives, such as reducing or delaying investments or capital expenditures, selling assets, refinancing or obtaining additional equity capital on terms that may be onerous or highly dilutive."
  • jump to section"Our business may not continue to generate cash flow from operations in the future sufficient to satisfy our obligations under our existing indebtedness and any future indebtedness we may incur, and to make necessary capital expenditures."
Topic 5 of 5

TSLA: debt

4 quarter(s) searched · 77 filing section(s) searched · 4 period(s) with evidence
Credibility timeline
Q1 2021Q2 2021Q3 2021Q4 2021
guidance met/exceededguidance missedguidance given, not yet resolvedno forecast data this quarter
Mention frequency
11
Q1 2021
12
Q2 2021
16
Q3 2021
11
Q4 2021

Longitudinal Summary

Over the course of 2021, the company consistently reported a reduction in its aggregate principal amount of indebtedness, decreasing from $9.52 billion at the end of Q1 to $5.38 billion by year-end. Management repeatedly emphasized progress in retiring legacy and high-interest debt, including the early settlement of significant notes such as the 2025 senior notes. The company also reclassified portions of convertible notes as they approached settlement dates. Interest capitalization and extinguishment charges were noted, reflecting accounting treatments related to debt. Throughout the year, the company maintained unused committed credit facilities and adapted its investment strategy to balance liquidity and risk.

Forecasting Credibility: None

No forward guidance on debt levels was provided by management, and thus no guidance versus actual pairs exist to assess forecasting credibility. All commentary on debt was retrospective or descriptive of actions taken rather than predictive. Therefore, it is not possible to rate management's forecasting credibility on this topic based on the available data.

Revenue & Long-Term Debt trend
Revenue ($10.39B → $13.76B) Long-Term Debt (% of revenue) (77.2% → 39.5%)
Q1 2021 – Q3 2021

Trends

  • steady reduction in total debt
  • retirement of high-interest legacy debt
  • reclassification of convertible notes
  • capitalization and amortization of interest
  • maintenance of unused credit facilities

Recurring Themes / Risks

  • legacy debt with higher interest rates
  • extinguishment charges on early debt repayments
  • management focus on debt reduction
  • liquidity management through credit facilities

Key Concerns

  • Extinguishment of debt charges (e.g., $60 million in Q3) may impact earnings and cash flow.
  • Reclassification of convertible notes indicates timing uncertainties in debt settlement.
  • No forward-looking guidance on debt limits ability to assess management's forecasting reliability.

Conclusion

The company demonstrated a clear and consistent effort to reduce its debt burden throughout 2021, successfully lowering aggregate principal indebtedness by over $4 billion. Management prioritized retiring higher-cost legacy debt, which should improve the company's interest expense profile and financial flexibility. The presence of extinguishment charges and reclassifications reflects active debt management but also introduces some accounting complexities. Despite the lack of forward guidance on debt, the actual reported reductions and management commentary indicate a disciplined approach to deleveraging. Overall, the company's debt position improved materially over the period, supporting a stronger financial foundation.

Evidence graph
  • Q1 2021
    • “Additionally, we are making progress reducing various forms of debt.”
    • ▼Long-Term Debt: $8.02B
    • ▼Short-Term Debt: $1.42B
    • ▼Total Debt: $9.43B
  • Q2 2021
    • “Additionally, each quarter, we are using our cash to retire legacy debt, which was taken on at a time when interest rates and company risk were much higher than in today's environment.”
    • ▼Long-Term Debt: $6.88B
    • ▼Short-Term Debt: $1.08B
    • ▼Total Debt: $7.96B
  • Q3 2021
    • “We also continue to retire high interest rate debt, including the early settlement of our 2025 senior notes of $1.8 billion during the quarter.”
    • ▼Long-Term Debt: $5.44B
    • ▲Short-Term Debt: $1.21B
    • ▼Total Debt: $6.65B
  • Q4 2021
    • ▼Long-Term Debt: $4.25B
    • ▼Short-Term Debt: $1.09B
    • ▼Total Debt: $5.34B
Executive tracker
Zachary Kirkhorn4 mentions

By Reporting Period

Q1 2021
View TranscriptView 10-K/10-Q

What management said

  • "Additionally, we are making progress reducing various forms of debt."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"As of March 31, 2021, we and our subsidiaries had outstanding $9.52 billion in aggregate principal amount of indebtedness, of which $1.43 billion is scheduled to become due in the succeeding 12 months."
  • from 10-K/10-Q"During the first quarter of 2021, the closing price of our common stock exceeded 130 % of the applicable conversion price of each of our 2022 Notes and 2024 Notes on at least 20 of the last 30 consecutive trading days of the quarter; causing the 2022 Notes and 2024 Notes to be convertible by their holders during the second quarter of 2021. As we now expect to settle a portion of the 2024 Notes in the second quarter of 2021, we reclassified $ 51 million of the carrying value of the 2024 Notes from debt and finance leases, net of current portion to current portion of debt and finance leases on our consolidated balance sheet as of March 31, 2021."
  • "Additionally, we are making progress reducing various forms of debt."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A

Risk Factors
  • jump to section"As of March 31, 2021, we and our subsidiaries had outstanding $9.52 billion in aggregate principal amount of indebtedness (see Note 10, Debt , to the consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q)."
  • jump to section"Furthermore, our failure to comply with our debt covenants could result in a default under our debt agreements, which could permit the holders to accelerate our obligation to repay the debt."
  • jump to section"We and our subsidiaries may, 48 subject to the limitations in the terms of our existing and future indebtedness, incur additional debt, secure existing or future debt or recapitalize our debt."
  • jump to section"If any of our debt is accelerated, we may not have sufficient funds available to repay it."
Q2 2021
View TranscriptView 10-K/10-Q

What management said

  • "Additionally, each quarter, we are using our cash to retire legacy debt, which was taken on at a time when interest rates and company risk were much higher than in today's environment."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"As of June 30, 2021, we and our subsidiaries had outstanding $8.03 billion in aggregate principal amount of indebtedness, of which $1.09 billion is scheduled to become due in the succeeding 12 months."
  • from 10-K/10-Q"As we now expect to settle a portion of the 2024 Notes in the third quarter of 2021, we reclassified $ 7 million of the carrying value of the 2024 Notes from debt and finance leases, net of current portion to current portion of debt and finance leases on our consolidated balance sheet as of June 30, 2021."
  • "Additionally, each quarter, we are using our cash to retire legacy debt, which was taken on at a time when interest rates and company risk were much higher than in today's environment."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A
  • jump to section"For details regarding our indebtedness, refer to Note 10, Debt , to the consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q."

Risk Factors
  • jump to section"As of June 30, 2021, we and our subsidiaries had outstanding $8.03 billion in aggregate principal amount of indebtedness (see Note 10, Debt , to the consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q)."
  • jump to section"Furthermore, our failure to comply with our debt covenants could result in a default under our debt agreements, which could permit the holders to accelerate our obligation to repay the debt."
  • jump to section"We and our subsidiaries may, subject to the limitations in the terms of our existing and future indebtedness, incur additional debt, secure existing or future debt or recapitalize our debt."
  • jump to section"If any of our debt is accelerated, we may not have sufficient funds available to repay it."
Q3 2021
View TranscriptView 10-K/10-Q

What management said

  • "We also continue to retire high interest rate debt, including the early settlement of our 2025 senior notes of $1.8 billion during the quarter."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"As of September 30, 2021, we and our subsidiaries had outstanding $6.70 billion in aggregate principal amount of indebtedness, of which $1.22 billion is scheduled to become due in the succeeding 12 months."
  • from 10-K/10-Q"In August 2021, we fully repaid the $ 1.80 billion in aggregate principal of the 2025 Notes and recorded an extinguishment of debt charge of $ 60 million related to the redemption."
  • from 10-K/10-Q"In March 2021, the 2021 Notes were fully settled."
  • from 10-K/10-Q"During the second and third quarters of 2021, we fully settled the warrants entered into in connection with the issuance of the 2021 Notes and partially settled the warrants entered into in connection with the issuance of the 2024 Notes, resulting in the issuance of 15.8 million and 18.7 million shares of our common stock, respectively."
  • from 10-K/10-Q"Automotive Asset-backed Notes and Warehouse Agreements In March 2021, we transferred beneficial interests related to certain leased vehicles into an SPE and issued $ 1.08 billion in aggregate principal amount of Automotive Asset-backed Notes, with terms similar to our other, previously issued, Automotive Asset-backed Notes. The proceeds from the issuance, net of discounts and fees, were $ 1.07 billion."
  • from 10-K/10-Q"Automotive Lease-backed Credit Facilities In June 2021, we fully repaid $ 32 million in aggregate principal of our Automotive Lease-backed Credit Facilities and terminated one of the facilities."
  • from 10-K/10-Q"Solar Revolving Credit Facility and other Loans In April 2021, we fully repaid the $ 67 million in aggregate principal of our Solar Revolving Credit Facility and the facility was terminated."
  • "We also continue to retire high interest rate debt, including the early settlement of our 2025 senior notes of $1.8 billion during the quarter."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A
  • jump to section"For details regarding our indebtedness, refer to Note 10, Debt , to the consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q."
  • jump to section"For details regarding our indebtedness, refer to Note 10, Debt to the consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q."
  • jump to section"These decreases were partially offset by an extinguishment of debt charge of $60 million related to the redemption of our 5.30% Senior Notes due in 2025."
  • jump to section"Cash Flows from Financing Activities Net cash used in financing activities during the nine months ended September 30, 2021 was $3.95 billion, which consisted primarily of $5.14 billion of net repayments under our various debt facilities and $311 million principal repayments of our finance leases ."

Risk Factors
  • jump to section"As of September 30, 2021, we and our subsidiaries had outstanding $6.70 billion in aggregate principal amount of indebtedness (see Note 10, Debt , to the consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this Quarterly Report on Form 10-Q)."
  • jump to section"Furthermore, our failure to comply with our debt covenants could result in a default under our debt agreements, which could permit the holders to accelerate our obligation to repay the debt."
  • jump to section"We and our subsidiaries may, subject to the limitations in the terms of our existing and future indebtedness, incur additional debt, secure existing or future debt or recapitalize our debt."
  • jump to section"If any of our debt is accelerated, we may not have sufficient funds available to repay it."
Q4 2021
View TranscriptView 10-K/10-Q

What management said

No high-confidence mentions this period - showing weaker, lower-confidence matches instead:

  • "In addition to using cash to grow the business as quickly as we can, we have been retiring legacy and high interest debt."

Guidance given

No forward guidance identified this period.

Actual reported

  • from 10-K/10-Q"As of December 31, 2021, we and our subsidiaries had outstanding $5.38 billion in aggregate principal amount of indebtedness (see Note 11, Debt , to the consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this Annual Report on Form 10-K)."
  • from 10-K/10-Q"In addition, we had $1.11 billion of unused committed amounts under our credit facilities as of December 31, 2021."
  • "In addition to using cash to grow the business as quickly as we can, we have been retiring legacy and high interest debt."

Filing disclosures (10-K/10-Q)

MD&A
  • jump to section"See Note 11, Debt , to the consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this Annual Report on Form 10-K for further details regarding our debt obligations."
  • jump to section"For details regarding our indebtedness, refer to Note 11, Debt to the consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this Annual Report on Form 10-K. We continue adapting our investment strategy to meet our liquidity and risk objectives, such as investing in U.S. government and other marketable securities, digital assets and providing product related financing."

Risk Factors
  • jump to section"As of December 31, 2021, we and our subsidiaries had outstanding $5.38 billion in aggregate principal amount of indebtedness (see Note 11, Debt , to the consolidated financial statements included elsewhere in this Annual Report on Form 10-K)."
  • jump to section"Furthermore, our failure to comply with our debt covenants could result in a default under our debt agreements, which could permit the holders to accelerate our obligation to repay the debt."
  • jump to section"We and our subsidiaries may, subject to the limitations in the terms of our existing and future indebtedness, incur additional debt, secure existing or future debt or recapitalize our debt."
  • jump to section"If any of our debt is accelerated, we may not have sufficient funds available to repay it."
